fragility_points: A broad crypto selloff before April 19 could erase the current cushion.
A Binance-specific downward dislocation at the settlement minute would matter even if other venues stay stronger.
key_assumptions: SOL does not fall roughly 6% from the mid-84s area into the April 19 12:00 ET settlement minute.
Binance SOL/USDT remains a representative and functioning settlement surface.
main_logical_chain: Verify the governing source and exact settlement mechanic from Polymarket rules.
Check Binance directly to measure current distance from the threshold on the governing venue.
Use outside-view short-dated crypto volatility logic to judge whether a roughly 6% drop by settlement is more likely than not.
Conclude Yes is favored but not close to certain because close-only mechanics preserve tail risk.
main_thesis: SOL is already trading materially above 80 on the governing venue, so the outside view favors a Yes resolution, though single-minute close mechanics preserve meaningful downside tail risk.
quote_anchors: The market resolves Yes only if the final close price for that exact minute is higher than 80.
source_quality_view: Source quality is fairly strong for a medium case: low ambiguity on settlement source, direct governing-venue verification, plus independent contextual spot checks.
strongest_disconfirmers: Crypto can move 6% in a few days without a special catalyst.
This contract depends on one exact 1-minute close, not an anytime touch or average price.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot was about 84.72, roughly 5.9% above the 80 threshold.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes were clustered around 84.67-84.75.
CoinGecko and Coinbase spot checks closely matched Binance, supporting the current cushion.
timing_relevance: This is a short-dated, timezone-sensitive contract tied to the April 19, 2026 12:00 ET Binance 1-minute close.
unresolved_ambiguities: No explicit historical volatility study was done for analogous SOL close-above contracts.
Threshold-close mechanics are not yet represented by a clean canonical driver slug.
what_would_change_view: A move back toward 80-81 on Binance, a broad crypto risk-off drop, or evidence of Binance-specific weakness would lower the estimate materially.
